name: Valsequillo — circular from Hinojosa del Duque
name_original: Valsequillo — circular desde Hinojosa del Duque
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Hinojosa Del Duque traverse a landscape characterized by extensive dehesas and natural areas. The region features diverse terrain, including gentle hills and valleys, with well-paved surfaces suitable for road cycling. Natural features such as reservoirs and open grasslands provide varied backdrops for routes. This area offers a mix of moderate and challenging routes for cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Hinojosa Del Duque The most popular road cycling route is Valsequillo — circular from Hinojosa del Duque, a 32.5 miles (52.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views of the Los Pedroches region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Monterrubio de la Serena from Bellacázar — circular via Cabeza del Buey, a moderate 55.0 miles (88.5 km) path. This route provides views of the dehesa landscape and varied terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Pozoblanco — circular from Villanueva del Duque, a 32.8 miles (52.8 km) trail leading through open grasslands, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Road cycling around Hinojosa Del Duque is defined by extensive dehesas, natural areas, and reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Hinojosa Del Duque are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 13 reviews. More than 390 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Hinojosa Del Duque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Hinojosa del Duque?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes available around Hinojosa del Duque, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a good mix of moderate and challenging options to explore the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Hinojosa del Duque?
A: The terrain around Hinojosa del Duque is characterized by extensive &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; (Andalusian grasslands), gentle hills, and valleys. Most routes feature well-paved surfaces, making them enjoyable for road cycling. You'll also encounter natural areas with reservoirs and open grasslands.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 13 difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016959451/almaden-from-belalcazar-circular-via-hinojosa-del-duque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almaden from Belalcazar — circular via Hinojosa del Duque&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 126 km with significant elevation gain, requiring very good fitness.

Q: Are there easier road cycling options for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride?
A: Absolutely. There are 5 easy routes and 20 moderate routes suitable for beginners or those preferring a more relaxed pace. A popular moderate route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016951781/valsequillo-circular-from-hinojosa-del-duque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valsequillo — circular from Hinojosa del Duque&lt;/a&gt;, a 52.4 km trail offering scenic views of the Los Pedroches region.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on road cycling routes in Hinojosa del Duque?
A: You'll primarily cycle through picturesque &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt;, which are a defining feature of the landscape. The routes also pass by various natural areas, including reservoirs like Pantano el Cascajoso and Embalse de la Colada, offering opportunities for bird watching and scenic views.

Q: Are there historical landmarks or points of interest to see while road cycling?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can incorporate visits to landmarks such as the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Juan Bautista of Hinojosa del Duque&lt;/a&gt; (Catedral de la Sierra), or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5311931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente El Pilar&lt;/a&gt;. Nearby, you might also spot &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5311931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belalcázar Castle&lt;/a&gt; offering panoramic views.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Hinojosa del Duque?
A: Many routes in the area are designed as circular loops, perfect for starting and ending in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016973219/monterrubio-de-la-serena-from-bellacazar-circular-via-cabeza-del-buey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monterrubio de la Serena from Bellacázar — circular via Cabeza del Buey&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016715918/pozoblanco-circular-from-villanueva-del-duque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozoblanco — circular from Villanueva del Duque&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Hinojosa del Duque?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant weather for road cycling in Hinojosa del Duque, with milder temperatures. Summers can be quite hot, while winters are typically mild but can have cooler days.

Q: Are there long-distance road cycling routes for endurance riders?
A: Yes, endurance riders will find several long-distance options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016959451/almaden-from-belalcazar-circular-via-hinojosa-del-duque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almaden from Belalcazar — circular via Hinojosa del Duque&lt;/a&gt; route, at 126 km, is a prime example for those looking for an extended ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy about the routes in Hinojosa del Duque?
A: The road cycling routes in Hinojosa del Duque are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 13 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the picturesque &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; landscapes, and the well-paved surfaces that make for an enjoyable ride.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Hinojosa del Duque?
A: Yes, as a municipality, Hinojosa del Duque offers various public parking options within the town and near popular starting points for routes, making it convenient for cyclists to access the trails.

Q: Are there any routes that follow historical paths, like the Mozarabic Route to Santiago?
A: Hinojosa del Duque is indeed located on the historic Mozarabic Route to Santiago. While specific road cycling routes may not follow the exact pilgrimage path, you can find routes that offer both scenic beauty and a sense of the region's historical significance, often passing by ancient hermitages and monuments.
tips: Hinojosa del Duque is a municipality in the province of Córdoba in the region of Valle de los Pedroches. Population known for Holy Week and its processions.

Embalse de la Colada fed by the Cascajoso stream is a relatively small swamp, built in 2006. To guarantee the water supply in the North of Córdoba and in the face of the 2022 drought, construction began in the same year of a connection to La Colada and Sierra Boyera.

It is a town located in the Valle del Guadiato region. At the entrance is an old olive press. Among the most beautiful buildings in the town are the town hall, which dates from 1949 and the parish of the Immaculate Conception from the 50s.
